title: Leilani M. Nelson - Brown Immigration Law
content:
Leilani M. Nelson joined Brown Immigration Law in February 2025 as a Senior Attorney handling various aspects of immigration law. Before joining Brown Immigration Law, Leilani gained substantial experience providing employment-based immigration solutions to various businesses and individuals across the United States. Her work centered around H-2A/H-2B Temporary Nonimmigrant Visas, O-1 Visas for Individuals with Extraordinary Ability or Achievement, National Interest Waivers, TN USMCA (formerly NAFTA) Visas, R-1 Nonimmigrant Religious Worker Visas, H-1B Specialty Occupation Visas, and Employment-Based Permanent Residence.
Throughout her career, Leilani also gained considerable experience handling immigration cases for advanced-degree individuals, multinational managers and executives, and skilled workers. Additionally, she represented clients in family-based applications and petitions, focusing on Form I- 130, Immediate Relative petitions, Form I-485, Adjustment of Status Applications, Consular Processing Applications, I-601/I-601A Waivers, and N-400, Naturalization Applications. She also has extensive experience working with victims of crimes (U Visas, victims of human trafficking (T Visas), and VAWA applicants.
Leilani graduated from Soka University of America (B.A., 2007) and received her law degree in 2011. After graduating from law school, she worked as a Program Associate for a Non-Governmental Agency affiliated with the United Nations, where she focused her attention on organizing programs on thematic issues, including women’s rights, sustainable development, nuclear non-proliferation, and disarmament. She is admitted to practice law in New York and is an active member of the American Immigration Lawyers Association.
Having been part of a family who immigrated to the United States, Leilani has developed a strong interest in helping individuals navigate through complex immigration laws with compassion and dedication.
- State Bar(s): New York State Supreme Court, First Appellate Division
- New York State Supreme Court, First Appellate Division
- Law school name, award(s), honors: Juris Doctor 2011 Soka University of America, Aliso Viejo, CA Bachelor of Arts – May 2007
- Soka University of America, Aliso Viejo, CA Bachelor of Arts – May 2007
- Bachelor of Arts – May 2007
- Sapon Immigration Law, Louisville, KY 2020-2025
- Law Offices of Usman B. Ahmad, Long Island City, NY 2017-2019
- Frink Hamlett Legal Solutions (Cognizant Technologies, Inc.), Teaneck, NJ 2016- 2017
- Ozisik, PLLC, New York, NY 2013-2016
